title: Storyline
summary: A broad outline of the current arc, its pressure points, and its reveals.
intro: A rough campaign skeleton meant to hold tone and momentum before every scene is locked.
facts: The opening should establish the group's dynamic before the larger problem fully surfaces.
Midgame pressure works best when familiar places come back changed.
The late reveal should clarify the mystery without flattening it.

sections.heading: Opening Beat
paragraphs: The story opens at the edge of familiar territory, where an ordinary objective starts to feel wrong in small, accumulating ways.
That first stretch should establish tone, group chemistry, and the shape of the mystery without rushing to explain everything.

sections.heading: Midgame Pressure
paragraphs: The middle act should push the player back through places they already know, only this time with more pressure, less certainty, and more to lose.
items: Camp upgrades unlock new strategies and dialogue.
Earlier allies start revealing conflicting goals.
Traversal routes become more dangerous or, occasionally, more open.

sections.heading: Late Reveal
paragraphs: The late reveal should make the earlier clues click into place, then force a choice that matters to the cast as much as it does to the wider world.
items: Clarify what the group misunderstood early on.
Resolve at least one relationship arc before the ending lands.
Leave just enough room for aftermath notes or future fallout.
